10.reason: [Missing space before %c causes operator to be read as a space, leading to infinite loop; use " %c" to skip whitespace]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Missing space before %c causes operator to be read as a space, leading to infinite loop; use " %c" to skip whitespace]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing space before %c causing operator to be read as whitespace, leading to infinite loop
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  The format string "%d%c" does not skip whitespace before reading the character. If there are spaces in the input (e.g., "10 +"), c2 will read a space instead of the operator '+'. This causes the inner while loop to be skipped incorrectly, and c2 (space) is assigned to c1. In the next iteration, scanf fails to parse an integer because it encounters the operator or space unexpectedly, leading to undefined behavior or an infinite loop if input buffering isn't handled as expected, causing Time Limit Exceeded. It should be " %c" or handle spaces.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The format string "%d%c" does not skip whitespace before reading the character. If there are spaces in the input (e.g., "10 +"), c2 will read a space instead of the operator '+'. This causes the inner while loop to be skipped incorrectly, and c2 (space) is assigned to c1. In the next iteration, scanf tries to read an integer but encounters the operator or space, leading to parsing errors or infinite loops if the input buffer isn't consumed correctly, resulting in Time Limit Exceeded or Wrong Answer. It should be " %d %c" or similar to handle spaces.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Missing space in format string causes %c to read whitespace (including space after number), leading to incorrect parsing and potential infinite loop]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The format "%d%c" does not skip whitespace, so if there's a space after the number, c2 will read the space instead of the operator, causing incorrect parsing and potentially infinite loop.]

12.reason: [Same issue: without space before %c, it reads a space instead of the next operator; use " %c"]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: without space before %c, it reads a space instead of the next operator; use " %c"]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Same issue: should be "%d %c" to skip whitespace before operator
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Similar issue: "%d%c" does not skip whitespace before reading c3. If there is a space after the number c, c3 will read a space instead of the next operator. This breaks the logic for chaining * or / operations.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  Similarly, this scanf does not skip whitespace before reading the character c3. If there are spaces after the number c, c3 might read a space instead of the next operator, breaking the logic for subsequent high-precedence operations.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: missing space before %c causes reading of unintended whitespace, disrupting operator detection]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: "%d%c" reads space as c3, leading to wrong operator detection and possible infinite loop.]
